ERROR U03 pin 16 ('VCC') tied to ground net 'GND' power_pin_miswire

U03 pin 16 is named 'VCC' (a supply pin) but is connected to 'GND', which the netlist tags as a GROUND net. This looks like a power/ground wiring mistake.

WARNING Redundant pull-up resistors on 'FAULT' redundant_pulls

2 pull-up resistors (R01, R02) are all connected to net 'FAULT'. Unless this is an intentional strength adjustment, one of them is likely redundant.

WARNING Resistor 051405R3 shared across 2 RC filter paths shared_rc_resistor

Resistor 051405R3 forms an RC low-pass with more than one capacitor (051405C12, 051405C6) on net(s) BLK0001/SIGN0017, SIG_PRIM_P, feeding ground net(s) GND, GND_IPSU. Sharing one resistor between separate filter paths couples them together and can create interference between signals that were meant to be filtered independently.

WARNING Resistor 051405R1 is a common source impedance for 3 independent RC branches shared_source_resistor

051405R1 (5.6 kOhm) feeds net 'BLK0001/SIGN0017', which fans out through 051405R3, 051405R4, 051405R8 into separately filtered branches (SIG_N, SIG_PRIM_N, SIG_PRIM_P, via 051405C1, 051405C6, 051405C7). Current drawn by either branch develops a voltage drop across 051405R1 that's visible to the other branch, coupling two measurements that were meant to be independent. If this is intentional (e.g. a deliberate summing/bias node), please confirm; otherwise each branch should be fed directly from the low-impedance source.

WARNING U03 pin 16 ('VCC') wired differently from sibling 74XX595 instances sibling_pin_mismatch

U03 pin 16 ('VCC') connects to net 'GND', but the other 2 instance(s) of the same part (74XX595) -- U02, U04 -- all connect their 'VCC' pin to net 'IPSU_5V'. This looks like a possible wiring/pin-swap mistake; verify against the schematic.

WARNING U04 pin 13 ('OE') wired differently from sibling 74XX595 instances sibling_pin_mismatch

U04 pin 13 ('OE') connects to net 'OUT2_IN3', but the other 2 instance(s) of the same part (74XX595) -- U02, U03 -- all connect their 'OE' pin to net 'ENABLE'. This looks like a possible wiring/pin-swap mistake; verify against the schematic.

WARNING U04 pin 8 ('GND') is not connected unconnected_power_pin

U04 pin 8 is named 'GND', but the netlist has no net connected to it at all. Unlike a spare GPIO, an unconnected supply/ground pin is usually a mistake.

INFO 6 decoupling caps on 'IPSU_5V' with no clear bulk capacitor excess_decoupling_no_bulk

Power net 'IPSU_5V' has 6 decoupling capacitors (081708C08, 08C01, C0001, C0005, C0006, C0007) but none stands out as a larger bulk/reservoir capacitor. This is fine for purely local decoupling, but worth double-checking that bulk capacitance exists somewhere on this rail.
